entries.problem_statement: SCE needs canonical scaffold and ownership extension semantics, but they should be delivered after preview/open and without creating adapter-owned ownership truth.
entries.expected_outcome: SCE exposes one canonical scaffold result and one ownership relation extension point without blocking phase-1 IDE projection work.
entries.impact_scope: engineering scaffold behavior, workspace relation semantics, later IDE/CLI onboarding enhancement
reproduction_steps: Observe that scaffold and ownership are mixed with preview/open semantics
Observe that this makes rollout order and implementation scope unstable
forbidden_workarounds: Do not make frontend store long-lived ownership truth to compensate
Do not let scaffold semantics block the first preview/open or Delivery projection rollout

entries.problem_statement: SCE needs one canonical engineering project preview and open/import result envelope so IDE and CLI stop inferring readiness from partial engineering commands.
entries.expected_outcome: SCE exposes one canonical preview payload and one open/import result envelope so adapters can display engineering readiness without guessing.
entries.impact_scope: app engineering projection, project preview/open flows, IDE and CLI engineering onboarding
reproduction_steps: Read existing engineering projection data for one app
Try to explain readiness and next action from separate attach/hydrate/activate states
Observe that adapters still need field synthesis and command-order inference
forbidden_workarounds: Do not solve readiness explanation by duplicating a frontend-owned registry
Do not mix scaffold/ownership fields into the preview/open contract

entries.problem_statement: SCE needs one canonical delivery projection read model for the IDE Delivery column instead of frontend heuristics across mixed files and evidence.
entries.expected_outcome: SCE publishes one scene-scoped delivery projection contract and command surface that both IDE and CLI can consume directly.
entries.impact_scope: scene delivery projection, handoff/release evidence reuse, IDE Delivery column integration
reproduction_steps: Attempt to render delivery-governance status from only existing task, handoff, and release files
Observe that object identity and scope back-links are not exposed through one canonical payload
Observe that frontend would need to synthesize long-lived delivery truth
forbidden_workarounds: Do not create a frontend-owned permanent delivery registry
Do not encode IDE layout semantics into the engine payload

entries.problem_statement: SCE needs a smaller, phaseable spec group for IDE delivery projection and engineering onboarding instead of broad mixed specs.
entries.expected_outcome: A smaller spec group exists so SCE can land the IDE Delivery column and engineering onboarding contracts in stable, phaseable steps.
entries.impact_scope: engine=scene/spec/task/app projection; consumer=frontend IDE and CLI adapters
reproduction_steps: Read the current broad draft specs for delivery projection and onboarding
Observe that delivery projection, preview/open, scaffold, and ownership are mixed across wide contracts
Try to pick one implementation order without additional decomposition
forbidden_workarounds: Do not let the frontend build permanent parallel truth from partial backend commands
Do not mix multi-agent runtime work into this phase-1 IDE projection task
